title: Carry the 155 pre-existing audit-chain breaks with a documented exception and a discriminated verifier; never silently re-hash
description: ISEDC D5, UNANIMOUS 7/7, the most-costly-to-recover-from decision. brain_audit_verify returns ok:false for 155 historical events (known hash-version migration artifact, 0 anchor breaks, new writes clean). Do NOT silently re-hash forward (that is the exact tamper event the threat model detects). Instead: (1) upgrade the verifier to emit 3 discriminated states verified/documented-exception(KNOWN_MIGRATION_ARTIFACT)/tamper-signature with newcomer-safe default output; (2) write a signed, dated, externally-anchored exception manifest (idx 24-2345, both dates, hash_version provenance); (3) any public showcase runs on a fresh post-migration brain. Only permitted re-baseline = one-time externally-anchored SIGNED cut-over preserving the old chain head.
design:
Pin the 155 exceptions by exact per-row tuple {id, entry_hash, prev_entry_hash, hash_version, seq}, byte-match classification only — NEVER by index-range 24-2345 or date (009-AT-DECR D5 step 3 wording must be amended). Extend scope beyond audit-verify.ts to also fix (a) the operator string at governed-second-brain-plugin/src/local-server.ts:170 ('TAMPER DETECTED' → 3-state) and (b) packages/eval-surface/src/provenance-integrity.ts:59 (hard-codes chainAnomalies===0, RED-forever on live brain, wired to no CI). Implement 3-state as a post-processor over existing verifyAuditChain breaks[], reusing the MergeAuditVerifyResult composite shape — do NOT fork the walk. Freeze manifest at exactly 155 (hard count-assert). NEVER re-hash (D5 unanimous). Must respect qmd-team-intent-kb/packages/store/src/audit-verify.ts (already discriminates CHAIN_FORK vs 3 tamper reasons) + audit-chain.ts (v1 hashes are the tamper record). Depends on e06.1 for the manifest anchor.

ASCII — the 155-breaks exception: safe (byte-pin) vs unsafe (range-key) classification (refs: /home/jeremy/000-projects/qmd-team-intent-kb/packages/store/src/audit-verify.ts, /home/jeremy/000-projects/governed-second-brain/000-docs/002-AT-DECR-epic1-deterministic-merge-gate.md):
  verifyAuditChain break  ->  e06.2 3-state classify
  --------------------------------------------------
  UNSAFE (009 D5 wording): key on idx 24-2345 / date
    attacker edits ANY row in-window  ->  break "expected"
    ->  laundered to KNOWN_MIGRATION_ARTIFACT  [FALSE-NEG]

  SAFE (mandate this instead):
    manifest pins each of 155 by exact tuple
    { id, entry_hash, prev_entry_hash, hash_version, seq }
    signed + externally anchored (same commit as chain head)
    ------------------------------------------------
    current stored hash == recorded tuple ? -> DOCUMENTED_EXCEPTION
    any drift from recorded broken-hash    ? -> TAMPER_SIGNATURE
    reason==CHAIN_FORK (own hash intact,           -> NOT auto-green;
      prev->real earlier row)                         needs anchor
                                                        chainedRows match

Remaining for e06.2: R5 (provenance-integrity: pass on tamperSignatures===0, disclose chainForks) + R8 (plugin brain_audit_verify: honest '0 tamper / 155 benign forks' summary) — consumers of the classifier.

title: Close the empty-host no-auth footgun so an empty TEAMKB_API_HOST cannot bind all interfaces while passing the loopback assertion
description: Adversarial verdict gapClosed=false. TEAMKB_API_HOST set to empty or whitespace passes isLoopbackHost (returns true for '') but flows to app.listen host '' which Node binds to all interfaces. NODE_ENV unset plus no tokens plus empty host equals unauthenticated admin reachable off-host. Trim and coerce empty/whitespace host to 127.0.0.1 at config load, or make isLoopbackHost('') return false.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true: loadConfig fed TEAMKB_API_HOST empty, whitespace, and undefined all resolve config.host to 127.0.0.1 via ?.trim() || default (the || is load-bearing; ?? was the bug); isLoopbackHost('') now false so a direct empty-host bypass trips the off-loopback boot refusal for an empty token registry. config.test.ts 5 cases pass, apps/api 276/276. Shipped on draft PR jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#205 (commit 72e56e1).

title: Auto-govern the remote brain_capture inbox nightly so teammate proposals promote instead of rotting
description: Auto-govern the remote brain_capture inbox nightly so teammate proposals promote instead of rotting.
notes:
⚠️ DESIGN CORRECTED after a 6-engineer Fable review (2026-07-09). The delete-based design above is WRONG — DO NOT BUILD IT. Verified flaws:
1. 005-AT-ARCH:65 classifies `candidates` as INSERT-ONLY / immutable / Tier-A NON-reproducible SOURCE OF TRUTH — the opposite of the 'transient staging' premise. Remote captures write NOWHERE else (no raw file, no spool): the candidate row is the ONLY copy of a teammate's proposal.
2. Delete-on-flagged/rejected destroys the human-review queue the system is built around (promotion-service.ts:28-30 'left untouched in the inbox so the admin can fix policy and retry') AND the only copy of the content; the reject audit event stores reason only, NOT content → admin retry 404s. Silent permanent loss of exactly the material policy said needs a human.
3. Delete breaks spool re-ingest idempotency: ingestFromSpool dedupes by findById against the candidates table; spool files are NEVER cleaned (~/.teamkb/spool holds every file since 06-22) → after a delete, next nightly re-inserts every spool candidate, re-rejects (new randomUUID audit event each night) → unbounded chain bloat. The proposed 'second run = no-op' test seeds the TABLE not spool files, so it would pass while missing this.
4. Curator dedupe is CROSS-TENANT (global getAllContentHashes / findByContentHash); promotion-service deliberately tenant-scopes to avoid a cross-tenant leak. Sweeping the whole inbox through the global path + delete institutionalizes + destroys.
5. 2186 hard deletes with ZERO receipts (duplicate path writes no audit); dangling candidateId back-links.

REVISED DESIGN (no DELETE): retire a governed candidate via a MARKER, keeping the row.
- Widen CandidateStatus (packages/schema/src/enums.ts:23 is a self-imposed z.literal('inbox'), not load-bearing) to inbox|promoted|rejected|flagged|duplicate + a store migration; OR add a nullable governed_at/last_outcome column the sweep skips.
- runGovern sweep: findByStatus('inbox', tenantId) → processBatch → transition ONLY {promoted,duplicate} to a terminal marker (row stays). Leave {flagged,rejected} in 'inbox' for admin disposal (preserves the review queue + content).
- Fix re-ingest idempotency independently: archive spool files after successful ingest (spool/ingested/) OR dedupe re-ingest by content_hash.
- Tenant-scope checkDuplicate/getAllContentHashes before the sweep ships (mirror promotion-service).
- Per-candidate try/catch containment in the sweep + a skip-and-report row mapper (rowToCandidate throws on one bad row today → aborts whole sweep forever).
- Emit ONE batch-level audit receipt per sweep (action:'governed', ids+outcomes, no content) so the operation is on the chain.
- Wrap promote() in db.transaction (promoter.ts is 5 autocommits → crash = memory with no receipt).
- Take the ~/.teamkb/.write.lock inside runGovern (the API + interactive local-mode writers currently escape it).
Cross-refs: EPIC-1-review findings R7/R8/R9. VERIFY on a throwaway brain. B1 must ship BEFORE kickoff (A5 depends on it) but ONLY in this corrected form.

AAR R7 = this bead (the corrected marker-based B1 design). Gate-1: B1 must ship AFTER R8 (server-side candidate override + attribution) and R9 (flock + promote transaction). See 012-AA-AACR.
B1 implemented as two paired PRs (marker-based, corrected design — NOT the delete design). INTKB store/curator primitives: jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#236 (CandidateStatus widened + migration #8 + findByStatus/updateStatus + tenant-scoped dedup + suppressRejectionReceipts + spool archive; validate green 1990 tests). Plugin runGovern inbox sweep + member-quarantine gate + batch 'governed' receipt + idempotency: jeremylongshore/governed-second-brain-plugin#20 (smoke/b1-inbox-sweep.mjs all 6 invariants green on a throwaway brain). PRs open, NOT merged; CI pending. Work done in isolated worktrees; live ~/.teamkb untouched.
close_reason: B1 DONE + deployed. Marker-based auto-govern of the remote inbox: widened CandidateStatus (inbox|promoted|rejected|flagged|duplicate|quarantined) + additive index-only migration #8; tenant-scoped findByStatus (skip-and-report row mapper) + tenant-scoped curator dedup; runGovern sweeps the inbox -> processBatch -> marks {promoted,duplicate} terminal, KEEPS {flagged,rejected} for admin, QUARANTINES member-authored (R8 marker) instead of auto-promoting; per-candidate containment; batch 'governed' receipt (no content); spool-archive for real idempotency. INTKB #236 + plugin #20 merged. TWO adversarial Fable reviewers PASS (reviewer 2 independently re-ran the smoke 18/18 + 22 unit tests + DB leak-dump); Gemini no blocking findings. Deployed in ORDER: INTKB v0.11.0 (sha 304b4ae, live API reads new statuses) THEN plugin sweep live (cjs on main). Non-blocking hardening -> jfv.2.5.

title: Try qmd OR-fallback/token-relaxed BM25 and re-run the retrieval eval before committing to the sqlite-vec semantic path
description: e06.4 measured semantic Recall@10=0.339, but qmd's default retrieval is strict-AND FTS5 (a doc is only returned if it contains ALL query tokens), so most semantic misses return literally ZERO rows — 0.339 is a FLOOR for this exact config, not pure BM25-vs-dense. Before committing to ~320MB of EmbeddingGemma-300M weights (0t9.3), run the CHEAP experiment: enable an OR-fallback / token-relaxed BM25 tokenization in the qmd-adapter and re-run pnpm eval:retrieval against governed-brain-v1. If OR-fallback lifts semantic recall materially, BM25 may still suffice and the semantic path stays deferred. Per the e06.4 agent's flagged caveat + 038-AT-DECR's two-part build trigger (eval-miss AND a real logged recall miss).
notes:
DEMAND-GATED on the same trigger as the sqlite-vec semantic path (0t9.3): run this cheap OR-fallback/token-relaxed BM25 experiment + re-run pnpm eval:retrieval BEFORE committing ~320MB of EmbeddingGemma weights. e06.4 already cleared half the 0t9.3 trigger (semantic Recall@10=0.339 < 0.85); when a real logged recall miss arrives (the other half), do THIS first — if OR-fallback lifts semantic recall materially, 0t9.3 may be unnecessary. Not part of the e06 ratified-adopt-list push; a follow-up surfaced by e06.4's strict-AND caveat.
Bumped to P1 + un-deferred 2026-07-10: now has a concrete dogfood-impact justification. Verified live on the founder's restarted session — the strict-AND FTS5 floor means a multi-term brain_search returns literally ZERO rows ('governance receipts hash-chained audit trail moat' -> 0 hits) while a single strong term works ('receipts' -> 3 qmd:// cited hits). On day one of the team dogfood that's a bad first impression: a teammate whose Claude passes a natural-language phrase to brain_search sees an empty result and assumes it's broken. Stopgap shipped: the onboarding page (demos.intentsolutions.io/bbb) smoke prompt + agent prompt now use a single strong term + a '1-2 keywords' tip. The OR-fallback/token-relaxed BM25 is the real fix so natural-language queries degrade gracefully. Cheap experiment (no new weights) per e06.4 + 038-AT-DECR.

title: Add governed freshness-on-push plus incremental compile, gated on a per-push inference-cost model
description: ISEDC D2-(a), P1. Adopt AutoWiki's on-push refresh (default-branch CI workflow; commit-hash-diff -> regenerate-only-affected; the deterministic UUID-v5/manifest-SHA spool makes 'only affected' safe) into /teamkb-compile + ICO compile. KEEP the govern+receipt on each delta (invariant: enters at candidates, full pipeline, no fast-path). GATE: attach a per-push inference-cost model BEFORE filing/building (a full compile is $50-200; on-push at 40 pushes/day is unbudgeted).
design:
Cost model must OUTPUT an enforceable ceiling + debounce/coalescing window, not just an estimate; re-derive $ on DeepSeek rates (the live provider per decisions.jsonl, NOT the .env 'anthropic' default). Preserve the content-stable spool candidate ID: regenerating an affected page must reproduce byte-identical uuidV5(namespace, workspaceId\x00relPath\x00bodySha256) — but note this is automatic from the on-disk bytes (packages/kernel/src/uuid.ts), so the real correctness risk is the diff logic wrongly marking a page unaffected (staleness §6.3), not ID divergence. Enter at candidates, full pipeline, NO fast-path. Serialize under a flock on ~/.teamkb with the nightly compile + backup (govern.ts steps 2-5 span DB+files+anchor non-atomically). Must respect intentional-cognition-os/000-docs/007-PP-PLAN-master-blueprint.md (§6.3 staleness), 010-AT-DBSC (compilations.stale flag), and packages/kernel/src/{uuid.ts,spool.ts}.

Acceptance: cost model OUTPUTS an enforceable per-UTC-day spend ceiling + a debounce/coalescing window (not just an estimate); $ re-derived on DeepSeek rates (live provider, NOT the .env 'anthropic' default); diff logic proven not to wrongly mark a page unaffected (byte-identical UUIDv5 differential); deltas enter at candidates, full pipeline, NO fast-path; serialized under the e06.12 flock.
close_reason: Governed freshness-on-push SHIPPED (ICO #154 merged, CLEAN — all gates green incl. the slow Stryker mutation-test). Incremental compile: only pages whose bodySha256 changed since last compiled_at are recompiled (deterministic UUID-v5 identity preserved → dedupe still holds), gated by a per-UTC-day cost ceiling + debounce, under a kernel write-lock so concurrent compiles serialize. Each delta still emits the full spool → INTKB govern → receipts (the freshness answer to AutoWiki's on-push, but with GSB's govern+receipt on every delta — the ratified adopt item #1). Gemini's 2 findings FIXED with the PREFERRED refactor: (1) HIGH — the 6 compile sub-passes called process.exit() which bypassed finally{closeDatabase(db)} (DB-handle leak); replaced with a thrown CompilePassError carrying exitCode, caught by the action handler which sets process.exitCode AFTER cleanup runs (+ same-class leak in the --daily-ceiling/--debounce parse guard); (2) MEDIUM — non-sargable cost-gate SQL → sargable compiled_at >= ? AND compiled_at < ? UTC-day range. Tests: compiler 578, cli 517, kernel 423 all green; escape-scan clean.

title: Build a govern-decision eval harness with per-check precision/recall on an adversarial labeled set
description: ISEDC D3-(f), P1. The 8 govern rules are verified-deterministic but UNEVALUATED for efficacy (a regex secret-scan can be deterministic AND miss a base64/split key). Build a labeled adversarial set (split keys, encoded tokens, PII in odd fields, known-negatives) and publish per-check precision/recall for secret/PII/dedup; gate promotion-pipeline changes on it the way retrieval is gated. Required before public 'deterministic govern catches X' claims (GC). Extends the qmd-team-intent-kb-0t9 eval epic (backend-agnostic).

title: Fix /teamkb-compile tenant mismatch: it ran as 'local' but the brain's 2054 memories live under 'intent-solutions'
description: The nightly compile job runs its governed-brain MCP in local mode with the DEFAULT tenant 'local', but all 2054 curated_memories in ~/.teamkb live under tenant 'intent-solutions'. Effects: (1) brain_search queries the empty qmd-index/local → 0 hits for everything, so search-before-save is blind and every candidate is marked 'new' (the 2026-06-29 digest self-flagged this); (2) brain_status counts all tenants → still shows 2054, masking it; (3) CRITICAL — in auto mode brain_capture/brain_govern would write into the 'local' tenant, a phantom brain, NOT the team brain. Verified fix: with TEAMKB_TENANT_ID=intent-solutions, brain_search returns real hits (beads dolt→4, backup→10) against the real index intent-solutions/cache/qmd/index.sqlite. Fix = bake TEAMKB_TENANT_ID=intent-solutions into the compile job's brain-mcp-config.json. Same bug class the 06-25 digest flagged.
notes: GitHub: intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#24 — https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain/issues/24. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note compile-then-govern-6zk` and `bd-sync close compile-then-govern-6zk` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
close_reason: Fixed + verified. Root cause: compile job ran tenant 'local' (default) but ~/.teamkb's 2054 memories are tenant 'intent-solutions' → brain_search hit empty qmd-index/local (0 hits everything), brain_status masked it (counts all tenants), and auto mode would have written to a phantom 'local' tenant. Fix: baked TEAMKB_TENANT_ID=intent-solutions into brain-mcp-config.json. Verified via config alone (no env): brain_search 'beads dolt'→4, 'backup'→10 against intent-solutions/cache/qmd/index.sqlite.

title: Build a nightly job that compiles the team's daily work into governed memories (/teamkb-compile)
description: The governed brain (~/.teamkb) only stays current when a human runs /brain-save; nothing auto-captures the team's daily work, so the brain drifts behind reality. Build a nightly local job — a /teamkb-compile skill plus a cron wrapper — modeled on the proven /blog-backfill pattern, that gathers the day's work signals (git commits, merged PRs, closed beads, decision records, Claude session transcripts) across ~/000-projects, lets Claude COMPILE them into durable governed-memory candidates, then runs the deterministic GOVERN path (brain_capture -> brain_govern: dedupe/policy/secret-scan/promote with a SHA-256 hash-chained receipt per decision). This is the Compile-then-Govern wedge applied to the team's own daily work: the model proposes, deterministic code disposes, with a receipt. Runs LOCALLY (not a cloud routine — the brain API binds the tailnet-only IP and data is local ~/.teamkb, which Anthropic-cloud routines cannot reach), nightly ~03:30 before the 04:30 backup so new memories land in that night's backup. Ships digest-first (emails a review digest for the first few days) then flips to auto-promote. Skill lives at ~/.claude/skills/teamkb-compile/; cron wrapper at ~/bin/teamkb-compile-daily.sh.
notes: GitHub: intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#15 — https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain/issues/15. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note compile-then-govern-bib` and `bd-sync close compile-then-govern-bib` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
close_reason: All 7 tracks shipped + verified. /teamkb-compile nightly governed-brain compile is LIVE (digest-first). Skill at ~/.claude/skills/teamkb-compile (SKILL.md + 5 references + memory-distiller agent + gather/transcript/mcp-config scripts + methodology/decisions.jsonl). Cron wrapper ~/bin/teamkb-compile-daily.sh @ 03:30 (before 04:30 backup). E2E proven: digest mode = gather→distill→search→digest+audit, no writes; auto mode = capture→govern→13 promoted with hash-chained receipts, brain_audit_verify intact. The Compile-then-Govern wedge applied to the team's daily work: the model proposes, deterministic code disposes, with a receipt.

title: Local-mode brain_search returns zero hits because it omits the tenant arg to the fail-closed adapter.query (regression from c5k.2)

title: Build the govern-at-merge gate as a post-merge re-derivation pass over the union that re-governs every merged row as untrusted
description: The decisive spike evidence: dolt merge admitted a secret row with zero governance. Build a post-merge re-derivation pass (dedupe, secret, disclosure, length with an accreting existingHashes set), proven commutative (A union B equals B union A byte-identical), that re-governs every merged row as untrusted. This is the layer Dolt structurally lacks.
close_reason: Govern-at-merge gate shipped + adversarially proven. Draft PR #207 (qmd-team-intent-kb, branch feat/epic1-govern-at-merge-gate, commit ca34659, NOT merged). mergeGovern() is a re-derivation-over-union pass reusing every existing primitive: union + id-dedup by content-derived UUIDv5, sort-by-id (the one new discipline = commutativity keystone), re-project each row to untrusted MemoryCandidate, assertDisclosureClean() fail-closed first, then full PolicyPipeline with accreting existingHashes seeded from getAllContentHashes(), promote survivors via canonical path with deterministic mergeClock so timestamps + audit chain re-walk identically across clones. Independent auditor (not reusing curator fixtures, called public mergeGovern entrypoint) confirmed gapClosed=true: (a) AWS-key/SSN/salary content rows AND a metadata-buried AWS key all QUARANTINED at the disclosure choke point, absent from durable curated_memories; (b) cross-clone content duplicate deduped to exactly one survivor by content-id; (c) A-union-B vs B-union-A byte-identical governed state (sha256 4ae3c0ee...) AND identical audit chain (sha256 28f0608a...), both verifyAuditChain breaks=0, byte-identity held even when the same union is partitioned across clones differently; (d) every survivor independently re-passed assertDisclosureClean + PolicyPipeline. Differential mutation testing proved tests non-vacuous (bypassing disclosure gate -> secrets rode in; admitting policy-rejected rows -> dedup probe failed), then source reverted byte-identical (sha256 match vs HEAD). Three smuggle vectors all FAILED (metadata free-text secret, content-id collision, reordering attack). 602 tests green; CI all-green (validate/gitleaks/semgrep). Working tree clean, on feature branch. EPIC 1 children 8da.7 (Ed25519 signed DAG anchor) + 8da.8 (merge-aware verifier owning canonical ordering) now build on this.

title: Build a merge-aware audit verifier that validates each clone chain plus the re-derived merged chain plus a signed DAG anchor
description: Build the merge-aware audit verifier: validate each clone chain in isolation, the re-derived merged chain, and a signed DAG anchor listing both parent heads. Dispatch on hash_version (v1 timestamp-in-hash vs v2 timestamp-excluded; the per-row dispatch already in audit-verify.ts is the template). OWNS cross-clone ORDERING reconciliation: define a canonical event ordering (by content-derived event id) so two clones that promoted the same set in different order reconcile to the same verified state - this resolves the ordering residual surfaced on 8da.6. The current linear verifier breaks at a merge boundary with PREV_LINK_MISMATCH.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true. Merge-aware verifier verifyMergeAuditChain: per-clone linear validation (ENTRY_HASH_MISMATCH on a tampered clone), verifier-owned canonical content-id ordering (canonicalMergeOrder, not gate-trusted), merged re-walk (PREV_LINK on a reordered merge), and signed DAG anchor cross-check (DAG_SIGNATURE_INVALID / DAG_PARENT_MISMATCH / DAG_HEAD_MISMATCH / DAG_ANCHOR_MISSING). 180/180 store tests, lint/typecheck clean. PR #208 commit 40fc800.

title: Wire the dead audit-anchor.ts into a per-actor Ed25519 signed chain-head with DAG-parent fields and a Lamport logical clock
description: audit-anchor.ts already exists but has zero production callers (the dead code from the tokens-auth finding). Wire it so the anchor binds chainHead plus commitHash plus logical clock plus parents[], signed per actor with native Node Ed25519, so cross-clone non-repudiation is real and not documentation-only.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true by an independent harness (real production modules, not the impl's own tests). Per-actor Ed25519 signed DAG merge anchor: parents[]=the two clone heads as an order-independent set, lamportClock, embedded signer public key, detached Ed25519 signature over the canonical merged head. The bare hash-chain was defeated by tamper-then-rehash (verifyAuditChain breaks:0) but the signed anchor CAUGHT it (HISTORY_REWRITTEN/DAG_HEAD_MISMATCH); a keyless forger failed (20,000 brute signatures, 0 accepted). No private key committed (ephemeral keys in tests, .gitignore blocks plaintext). 180/180 store tests. PR #208 commit c8de18e.

title: Make audit-chain timestamps deterministic so two clones promoting the same logical event produce identical entry_hash
description: The single most load-bearing EPIC 1 change. audit-chain.ts hashes the timestamp into entry_hash, so wallclock new Date() makes two clones produce different hashes for the same logical event. Derive the canonical-row timestamp from candidate.capturedAt or exclude timestamp from the canonical hash. Requires a canonical-row rehash migration, not a drop-in.
notes: Design call (steering): the v2 entry_hash is intentionally chain-position-dependent (it folds in prev_entry_hash) - correct for a hash chain. The foundation invariant is therefore 'same ordered event set -> same chain head; same logical event with a deterministic id at the same position -> same entry_hash', NOT per-event order-independence. Cross-clone reconciliation of differently-ordered chains is delegated to the merge-aware verifier 8da.8. title: Scope + build the teammate auto-capture hook: session-end Stop hook that distills each teammate's Claude session and brain_captures the learnings into the team brain (B3)
description:
GOAL: extend the 'my work auto-fills the brain' loop (Jeremy's 3:30am teamkb-compile — the day-scoped analog of /blog-backfill) to EACH teammate, so the WHOLE team's daily work flows into the one team brain automatically, not just Jeremy's. Closes the last capture gap: today the compile scans Jeremy's box only (his clones + his transcripts; teammates' SHIPPED work is swept all-authors from repos he clones), but teammates' RAW local sessions on their own machines never reach the brain unless they manually /brain-save.

DESIGN (per-machine hook path): a Claude Code Stop/SessionEnd hook shipped opt-in with the plugin, installed in the teammate's ~/.claude/settings.json. On session end it (1) reads the just-finished transcript; (2) runs the same memory-distiller the nightly compile uses to extract capture-worthy candidates (decisions/gotchas/learnings, NOT raw chatter); (3) calls brain_capture (team mode -> remote brain API) per candidate; (4) candidates land in the remote inbox -> governed overnight by B1 (auto-govern-inbox, already built marker-based) -> searchable next day with receipts. Manual counterpart already ships: /brain-save. The hook is the automatic version.

HARD PREREQUISITE: the REMOTE brain_capture write path. Team mode currently proxies ONLY brain_search (read) to the remote brain (src/remote-server.ts); remote brain_capture over the API is NOT live yet (the 650.2 / remaining team-mode surface work). The hook is BLOCKED on this — without it, captures can't reach the remote brain. Also needs B1 (built).

RISKS/CONSIDERATIONS: noise+privacy (auto-capturing every session risks noise + sensitive/half-baked content -> mitigate: distill to real decisions only, server-side govern dedupe/policy/secret-scan, MEMBER trust-level lands untrusted->reviewed per R8 intake override, digest-first rollout before auto-promote); opt-in consent per teammate, never forced; token cost (distiller runs Claude per session on the teammate's plan); enforcement-travels-with-code (hook ships with the plugin).

ALTERNATIVE (central, no per-machine hook) — DECISION PENDING: if the Claude Team/Enterprise plan exposes teammates' session transcripts centrally to the paying admin, ingest everyone's sessions CENTRALLY on Jeremy's box (feed the existing nightly compile) instead of per-machine hooks — simpler, zero teammate setup. Gated on a claude-code-guide finding (in flight 2026-07-10: does a team/enterprise plan give the admin central access to members' Claude Code session transcripts?). If central access exists -> prefer it; if not -> the per-machine hook is the only path.

WEBSITE: demos.intentsolutions.io/bbb now has a 'want it to remember automatically?' section pointing teammates to ask Jeremy for the hook, plus a /brain + /brain-save slash-command guide.
notes:
DECISION RESOLVED (2026-07-10, claude-code-guide agent, well-sourced): the 'central ingest via team plan' alternative is RULED OUT. Claude Code session transcripts are stored LOCAL-ONLY on each machine (~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl) and are NOT accessible to org admins on EITHER Team or Enterprise — by architectural design. The Enterprise Compliance API covers claude.ai WEB chats/files/projects (server-side) but EXPLICITLY EXCLUDES Claude Code sessions. Admin features that DO exist are metadata-only (audit/activity feed = who/what/when no content; usage analytics = tokens/cost; managed policies = behavior control; spend caps). Only opt-in exception: a user can individually choose 'yes Anthropic can view this session' via the feedback prompt — uploads to ANTHROPIC not the admin, per-user/per-session, cannot be mandated. => The PER-MACHINE Stop hook is the ONLY path to centralize teammates' local work; paying for their seats does NOT grant transcript access. This is not a workaround — it's the correct pattern given Claude Code's deliberately local-only storage; the governed brain's per-machine-capture + central-governed-brain + receipts is the right architecture. Docs: code.claude.com/docs/en/sessions#where-transcripts-are-stored, /data-usage, platform.claude.com/docs/en/manage-claude/compliance-api. Remaining blocker stays: the REMOTE brain_capture write path (team mode is read-only today).
CORRECTION (2026-07-10, grounded code research): the 'hard prerequisite: remote brain_capture write path' is ALREADY SHIPPED — not a blocker. remote-server.ts:224-313 registers brain_capture + brain_transition (landed 49cb8ef '650.2 #8', hardened 83690d4/R4); POST /api/candidates applies R8 override server-side (candidate-service.ts:70-106, tested); disclosure/secret scan runs pre-insert (:166-177 + candidate-repository.ts:218-223); B1 sweepInbox governs remote captures nightly, member-authored -> QUARANTINED never auto-promoted (govern.ts:268-273); sqlite-free invariant intact. So a teammate /brain-save ALREADY reaches the governed brain today. The hook's real prereqs are the gap beads: approve-from-quarantine tool + capture-path hardening (idempotency/outbox). A member capture with NO admin-approve surface would just pile up quarantined — fix that first.
Track 4 SHIPPED as PRs: plugin jeremylongshore/governed-second-brain-plugin#29 (opt-in Stop hook hooks/session-end-capture.mjs + consent-gated hooks/enable-autocapture.mjs + guard smoke 5/5 — 3 hard guards, detached, NEVER a plugin.json hook so install ≠ enable) + umbrella buy-in doc intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#40 (000-docs/015-AT-RNBK, rollout gated on consent). Depends on jfv.9 (idempotency+outbox, done). Hook BUILT + off-by-default; live enablement is per-teammate opt-in.
close_reason: MERGED (BUILT, opt-in, NOT auto-installed): plugin#29 (Stop hook + consent-gated enable flow, 3 guards, team.json fallback + robustness after 7 review findings) + umbrella#40 buy-in doc (015-AT-RNBK). Live activation is per-teammate opt-in (deferred by design).

title: Close the govern-eval-surfaced secret-scan evasions: split-across-newline keys and base64-wrapped tokens
description: SURFACED BY the govern-decision eval (compile-then-govern-e06.3, packages/eval-surface/src/govern-decision). Both the line-based scanForSecrets (claude-runtime/secrets/secret-scanner.ts, splits on \n and matches per-line) and the boundary scanForDisclosure (common/disclosure-filter.ts) MISS: (1) a credential split across two lines (cases sec-split-openai-01, sec-split-aws-01); (2) a base64-wrapped token (sec-b64-openai-01, sec-b64-github-01). These are documented false-negatives in the v1 dataset (knownFalseNegativeOf), so the eval PASSES today but the gaps are real — the CISO's exact fear (a leaked key promoted with a clean receipt). Fix candidates: a windowed / newline-collapsed pre-pass before the line scan; a bounded base64-decode-and-rescan pass on high-entropy blobs. When fixed, flip the case labels from knownFalseNegativeOf to expectCaughtBy and bump DATASET_VERSION (the eval will then require the catch).
notes: PR open (do not merge yet): jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#222. Closed both scan evasions in claude-runtime scanForSecrets — (A) newline-collapsed pre-pass rejoins split-across-newline keys, (B) bounded base64/hex decode-and-rescan catches encoded-wrapped tokens (base64-wrapped:/hex-wrapped: ids), with a printable-decode gate so benign base64 (data-URI images) does NOT false-positive. Relabelled the 4 now-caught cases (sec-split-openai/aws, sec-b64-openai/github) from knownFalseNegativeOf to expectCaughtBy and bumped govern-decision DATASET_VERSION 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0. Re-measured via govern:ci (green in CI): secret-scanner recall 0.5625 -> 0.7500; cascade lifts policy-pipeline 0.5789 -> 0.7368 and content-classifier 0.6364 -> 0.7727; mean F1 0.7333 -> 0.8188; zero undocumented false-negatives; precision held. Also fixed a PRE-EXISTING red gitleaks gate (allowlist covered 3 of ~15 fixture test files) by scoping the allowlist to __tests__/ + the dataset (negative-control verified: real key in a production path still caught). Full validate (1904 tests) + govern:ci + gitleaks all green on #222; NOT merged.
close_reason: Secret-scan evasions CLOSED (INTKB #222 merged). scanForSecrets now has 3 additive passes (per-line untouched): (A) newline-collapsed pre-pass (rejoins a key split across a newline), (B) bounded base64/hex decode-and-rescan (printable-gated, DoS-capped) catching encoded-wrapped tokens. MEASURED: secret-scanner recall 0.5625->0.7500 (P=0.9231), policy-pipeline recall 0.58->0.74, content-classifier 0.64->0.77, mean F1 0.73->0.82; govern:ci passes (zero undocumented false-negatives, DATASET_VERSION 1.1.0). Zero new false-positives (data-URI PNG + prose blob don't trip; isMostlyPrintable gate). Gemini review addressed (regex lastIndex reset at both call sites; index-accurate decode locate). Also fixed a pre-existing red gitleaks gate (test-fixture allowlist). Documented follow-ups: converge the common boundary scanForDisclosure (same evasions) + metadata/tenant scanning. Closes the CISO's exact fear (a split/encoded key promoted with a clean receipt), now measurably harder.

title: Build the govern phase: run each candidate through brain_capture then brain_govern with receipts and reject-with-reason

notes: GitHub: intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#19 — https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain/issues/19. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note compile-then-govern-bib.4` and `bd-sync close compile-then-govern-bib.4` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.

Confirmed runtime auto-seeds default policy (govern.ts:100, idempotent). LIVE headless smoke (TEAMKB_BASE_PATH=/tmp/teamkb-test, --mcp-config --strict-mcp-config): 2 captured → brain_govern {ingested:2,promoted:2,rejected:0,indexUpdated:true,anchored} → brain_audit_verify intact (2 events, 1 anchor, 0 breaks); teamkb.db has 2 curated_memories + 2 audit_events + exported markdown.

title: Harden the deployed brain API: give tokens an expiry/rotation, allow an anonymous health probe, and plan the move to a dedicated tailnet VM
description: Deploy follow-ons found verifying 650.5. (1) tokens.json records have expiresAt:null — the c5k registry supports expiry; set rotation/expiry for jeremy+ope. (2) /healthz requires auth (401), so a tailnet uptime probe can't hit it anonymously — add an unauthenticated health route (partner-portal pattern). (3) D27: the API runs on the dev box team-server for the soak; migrate to a small dedicated tailnet VM once load-bearing (the dev box also runs Claude sessions + has OOM history).
notes:
SECURITY (highest item here): tokens.json stores bearer secrets in PLAINTEXT on disk — jeremy+ope records are not scrypt$-prefixed, so the plaintext bearer tokens sit in ~/.teamkb/tokens.json (mode 600) and any borg backup of ~/.teamkb. The registry already accepts pre-hashed 'scrypt$salt$hash' records — re-seed with hashToken() output (or SOPS-encrypt the file) so no plaintext token lives on disk.
Decomposed into the team-rollout hardening epic E: E1 (token hashing = the plaintext-on-disk item), E2 (anon health — already live), E3 (dedicated tailnet VM decision). This bead is superseded by that decomposition; close it when E1 ships and E3 is decided.

title: Re-assert enum-field membership at the repository layer so a raw insert caller cannot smuggle disclosure content through a closed-vocab field
description: Defense-in-depth follow-up from the c5k.1 adversarial probe. collectFreeTextFields() skips ENUM_CONSTRAINED_FIELDS (status, source, category, trustLevel, confidence, sensitivity, type) by name, so a RAW caller planting an SSN-shaped value under category/status/source/author.type bypasses the disclosure scan and insert() writes it raw (count==1). UNREACHABLE in production (apps/curator import-pipeline and spool-intake hardcode these from closed vocab), but a future raw caller forwarding untrusted enum values without a Zod parse would re-open it. Fix: re-assert MemoryCandidate.parse() or enum membership at the repo layer, or scan enum fields that FAIL their closed-vocab check. LOW severity, not blocking PR #205.
close_reason: enum-membership shipped + merged in PR #209 (adversarially verified); re-close after jsonl-autoimport reverted it

title: Upgrade brain_search to qmd hybrid query (auto-expansion + rerank) plus an embed step in govern, for natural-language recall
description: brain_search currently runs qmd search (BM25, AND-semantics) which is brittle for natural-language queries: hyphenated/compound terms (e.g. 'in-process') tokenize to zero matches and fail the AND, so verbose questions often return nothing. qmd query (hybrid: auto-expansion + vector + LLM rerank) handles NL far better but needs qmd embed (a one-time ~333MB model download + embedding pass). Decide whether to add an embed step to brain_govern and switch brain_search to qmd query. Shared improvement (the internal brain uses the same BM25 adapter).
notes: Empirical confirmation (this session): qmd hybrid 'query' with the full NL question 'does the brain run in-process without a daemon' → 1 hit, while BM25 'search in-process' → 0. The upgrade path (switch brain_search to qmd query + add an embed step in govern) fixes the natural-language recall gap. Cost: ~333MB embedding model + one embed pass per govern.
close_reason: Folded into the qmd-team-intent-kb-0t9 native-retrieval-backend epic per user decision 2026-06-17 ('defer into 0t9'). brain_search semantic recall will land via 0t9's on-device sqlite-vec/hybrid backend, NOT a standalone qmd-hybrid + ~333MB-qmd-embedding-model path the native backend would supersede (avoids throwaway work; keeps the plugin's footprint light). Current BM25 cited (qmd://) search stays as-is until then. Scope addendum recorded on qmd-team-intent-kb-0t9.

title: Fix the govern-eval-surfaced precision leak: the heroku-api-key rule is a bare UUID regex that over-blocks benign UUIDs
description: SURFACED BY the govern-decision eval (compile-then-govern-e06.3). The heroku-api-key pattern in claude-runtime/secrets/patterns.ts is a bare UUID regex, so ANY UUID in prose (case neg-uuid-in-prose-01) is flagged as a credential by scanForSecrets and classifyContent, dragging secret-scanner precision to 0.90, content-classifier to 0.93, and causing the policy pipeline to over-REJECT a benign note. The boundary filter (no UUID rule) stays at precision 1.0. This is a false-positive / over-block, not a leak — but it will reject legitimate memories that mention a request id / trace id. Fix candidates: gate the UUID rule behind a key-context keyword (e.g. requires 'heroku'/'api'/'key' nearby), or drop the rule. When fixed, the eval's per-check precision rises toward 1.0. Also worth folding in: converge the claude-runtime classifier PII vocabulary (email/phone/SSN) with the boundary filter's (adds DOB / background-check) so a DOB-only leak does not pass the policy pipeline (case pii-inline-dob-01).
close_reason: Govern precision leak FIXED (INTKB #224 merged, CLEAN). Two deterministic (below-the-spool, no model call) fixes: (1) the heroku-api-key bare-UUID rule was flagging ANY UUID in prose as a live credential → over-REJECT of benign memories; added SecretPattern.requiresContext so the UUID rule fires ONLY with key-context nearby (heroku/api key/token/HEROKU_API_KEY= assignment), enforced uniformly across all 3 scanner passes (per-line, newline-collapsed, base64/hex-decode) via execWithContext — the rule was GATED not dropped, so a real Heroku key is still caught; (2) converged classifyContent's PII vocab UP to the boundary filter's (added date-of-birth/ssn-keyword/background-check) so a DOB-only leak is caught. EVAL (dataset v1.1.0→v1.2.0, govern:ci): precision → 1.0000 on ALL THREE in-content checks (policy-pipeline 0.9333→1.0, secret-scanner 0.9231→1.0, content-classifier 0.9444→1.0); boundary-filter HELD at 1.0; ZERO undocumented false-negatives. RECALL HELD + TESTED not assumed: added sec-inline-heroku-01 (real Heroku key in HEROKU_API_KEY= context) — still fired; neg-uuid-in-prose-01 now passes; pii-inline-dob-01 now caught. Gemini HIGH (real recall regression the gate introduced — the decode-rescan pass gated on the DECODED blob so a base64/hex-WRAPPED Heroku key WITH context would be silently missed): FIXED by threading contextText so the gate evaluates the ORIGINAL content while the value regex runs on the decoded blob; both directions pinned by tests. 1946 tests/163 files pass; typecheck/lint/prettier/depcruise/crap/harness-pin/provenance:ci/govern:ci green; Codecov all-covered.

title: Keep the brain's qmd search index fresh — it drifts when team-API writes bypass the in-process export

The team-server INTKB API writes memories straight to the DB (+ the trigger-maintained native FTS5 table curated_memories_fts) but never runs that export path — so kb-export froze at the 2026-06-14 bulk load (680 files) while the DB grew to 2054 (1374 memories from 06-15..22 invisible to qmd search, though present in native FTS5). DURABLE options: (1) the nightly /teamkb-compile job in AUTO mode runs brain_govern nightly, which re-exports -> keeps qmd fresh automatically (primary mitigation, active once auto-graduated); (2) point brain_search at the native FTS5 backend (curated_memories_fts, always current via triggers; the 0t9.2 native backend) so search never depends on export freshness. Pick one as the durable answer.

GitHub: intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#26 — https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain/issues/26. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note compile-then-govern-ugj` and `bd-sync close compile-then-govern-ugj` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
